name: wireshark
title: wireshark
description:
network traffic analyzer - graphical interface
Wireshark is a network "sniffer" - a tool that captures and analyzes
packets off the wire. Wireshark can decode too many protocols to list
here.

commands.name: wireshark
commands.description: Interactively dump and analyze network traffic

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use wireshark because network traffic analyzer - graphical interface.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use wireshark as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wireshark  [  -i  <capture  interface>|-  ]  [  -f  <capture filter> ] [ -Y <display filter> ]` as documented upstream for wireshark.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wireshark -h|--help` as documented upstream for wireshark.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wireshark -v|--version` as documented upstream for wireshark.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair wireshark with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `wireshark --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: wireshark - Interactively dump and analyze network traffic
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install wireshark. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `wireshark  [  -i  <capture  interface>|-  ]  [  -f  <capture filter> ] [ -Y <display filter> ]`. Further invocations from upstream: `wireshark -h|--help`; `wireshark -v|--version`. network traffic analyzer - graphical interface.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: network traffic analyzer - graphical interface.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: wireshark - Interactively dump and analyze network traffic.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install wireshark
